listingName: Portland Airport Hyatt Dual Brand Conversion

reasons.title: Dual-Brand Hyatt Strategy
reasons.description: Vesta is transforming a former Shilo hotel into a dual-brand Hyatt House and JDV by Hyatt, capturing both upscale extended-stay and lifestyle boutique market segments.

reasons.title: Distressed Asset Basis
reasons.description: Acquired through foreclosure at a total cost basis significantly below current replacement cost, providing immediate value creation potential through renovation.

reasons.title: Prime Transit-Oriented Location
reasons.description: Located five minutes from Portland Airport (PDX) and the I-205 interchange, benefiting from 20 million annual travelers and high sub-market occupancy.

summary.quote: The property presents a compelling repositioning and rebranding opportunity, situated within an Opportunity Zone in a strong sub-market of the Portland area.
paragraphs: Vesta Hospitality is seeking $10.5 million of equity as part of the total capitalization of $63.3 million for the renovation and repositioning of its all-suites hotel at Portland Airport. The requested funds will be allocated to a redevelopment budget of $2.8 million and to the early repayment of a mezzanine loan principal along with estimated accrued interest of $7.7 million.

A Vesta Hospitality-sponsored entity bought the property in June 2024 from a lender who foreclosed on it in late 2023. Formerly a Shilo hotel, it now operates independently as Sojourn Suites on an interim basis.
summary.conclusion: Vesta anticipates attractive returns with a projected 8% plus cash-on-cash return starting in 2027 and IRR (with sale proceeds) of 14.1% for the new investors.

cards.title: Financial Returns
cards.summary: The investment offers an 8% cumulative preferred return with robust cash flow projections starting in 2027 following the completion of renovations and Hyatt brand activation.

cards.title: Property Overview
cards.summary: A comprehensive transformation of a 202-room all-suite asset into a dual-branded Hyatt facility, featuring a standalone restaurant, ballroom, and 296 parking spaces.

cards.title: Market Analysis
cards.summary: The PDX sub-market is supply-constrained with 73.5% trailing occupancy in the upscale segment, and airport traffic is projected to grow to 35 million annual visitors.

cards.title: Sponsor Profile
cards.summary: Founded in 1996, Vesta Hospitality is a veteran Pacific Northwest firm with extensive experience in rebranding, development, and high-performance hotel operations.

financialReturns.pageSubtitle: Detailed financial projections and investment structure

projections.label: Target IRR (2026)
projections.description: Targeted internal rate of return for new 2026 equity investors.

projections.label: Preferred Return
projections.description: Cumulative non-compounding annual return paid before profit splits.

projections.label: Stabilized Cash-on-Cash
projections.description: Expected annual cash return on investment starting in 2029.

projections.label: Exit Cap Rate
projections.description: The capitalization rate applied to trailing NOI at the time of sale.

projections.label: Total Project Cost
projections.description: Total estimated budget for acquisition, renovation, and reserves.

projections.label: Loan-to-Cost (LTC)
projections.description: The ratio of senior debt to total project costs.

uses.description: Purchase price of the former Shilo hotel property
Reserves for operations during repositioning phase
Acquisition fees, loan fees, closing costs, and working capital

timeline.description: Hotel is under renovation and operates as Sojourn Suites. No distributions expected.
Hyatt brands open; operations ramp up. Distributions commence.
Stabilized operations under Hyatt House and JDV brands.

benefits.title: Capital Gains Deferral
benefits.description: Allows investors to reinvest capital gains into the fund to defer taxes.

benefits.title: Accelerated Depreciation
benefits.description: Significant accelerated depreciation of improvements available for 2026 investments.

benefits.title: Tax-Free Appreciation
benefits.description: Permanent exclusion from taxable income of capital gains if held for 10+ years.

saleWaterfall.description: Payment of any unpaid 8% preferred return balances.
Repayment of the full initial equity investment.
Final split of all remaining sales proceeds as incentive for the sponsor.

cashFlowDistribution.description: Paid until investors reach an 8% cumulative non-compounding return.
All remaining cash flow goes to pay down the initial capital investment.
Split between investors and the sponsor after capital and pref are paid.

propertyOverview.pageSubtitle: Physical asset details and site characteristics

facts.label: Site Size
facts.description: Total land area in Opportunity Zone

specialFeatures.title: Full-Service Conversion Finishes
specialFeatures.description: The project features a $38.9M renovation budget ($192.7k/key) covering a complete interior and exterior transformation of all-suite oversized rooms.

highlights.title: Airport Proximity
highlights.description: Located only five minutes from Portland International Airport (PDX).

highlights.title: Opportunity Zone Benefits
highlights.description: Eligible for permanent exclusion from taxable income on capital gains if held for 10 years.

highlights.title: Strategic Connectivity
highlights.description: Immediate access to the I-205 interchange serving the Portland/Vancouver metro.

marketAnalysis.pageSubtitle: Local economic drivers and competitive landscape

metrics.label: Metro Population
metrics.description: Total population for the Portland Metropolitan area.

metrics.label: Unemployment Rate
metrics.description: Reported unemployment rate as of August 2025.

metrics.label: Market Occupancy (Upscale)
metrics.description: Trailing twelve-month occupancy for upscale chains in the sub-market ending Oct 2025.

metrics.label: Industrial Vacancy Rate
metrics.description: Direct vacancy rate for the 84.35M SF NE Portland industrial base (Q3 2025).

metrics.label: Airport Traffic Growth
metrics.description: Year-to-date passenger traffic increase at PDX airport as of October 2025.

metrics.label: Submarket ADR
metrics.description: Average Daily Rate for upscale chains in the sub-market (TTM Oct 2025).

drivers.title: Airport Expansion
drivers.description: The Portland airport currently handles 20 million visitors annually, with projections to reach 35 million over the next 20 years.

drivers.title: Industrial Sector Strength
drivers.description: NE Portland maintains an 84.35M SF industrial base with a low 6.2% vacancy rate, driven by e-commerce and manufacturing.

drivers.title: Corporate Employment Base
drivers.description: A robust employment base led by Providence Health, Intel, and OHSU supports consistent corporate demand.

drivers.title: Limited New Supply
drivers.description: The sub-market is significantly built out, creating high barriers to entry for new hospitality competition.

analysis.title: Barriers to Entry
analysis.description: The airport sub-market is substantially built out, presenting limited opportunities for new hotel inventory to enter the market.

analysis.title: Long-term Demand Growth
analysis.description: Annual airport visitors are expected to grow from 20 million to 35 million over the next 20 years.

analysis.title: Industrial Market Stability
analysis.description: The industrial sector maintains high absorption with 84.35M square feet of space and only 6.2% vacancy.

data.summary: The airport sub-market is characterized as substantially built out with limited opportunity for new competitors. The existing Hyatt Place represents the only current Hyatt brand in the sub-market, maintaining high occupancy levels.

sectors.title: Technology & Software
sectors.description: Over 60,000 software and technology jobs within the Portland MSA, featuring major global firms.

sectors.title: Industrial & Logistics
sectors.description: A massive local base of 84.35 million square feet with high occupancy (93.8%).

sectors.title: Healthcare & Life Sciences
sectors.description: Major regional medical systems including OHSU and Providence Health serve as the region's largest employers.

paragraphs: Vesta Hospitality is a results-driven hotel management and development firm founded in 1996. The company is built on a foundation of strong partnership relationships and a demonstrated record of achieving improved operating results through property renovations, product quality, and personnel training.

The firm's executive team averages over 30 years of hospitality experience each, spanning large-scale development, asset management, and complex public-private partnerships. Vesta is uniquely positioned in the Portland/Vancouver sub-market, having operated local airport-adjacent assets for over two decades.

items.text: Managed 47 hotel properties across North America since 1996.
Hospitality-focused firm specializing in distressed asset repositioning.
Current portfolio achieves a 123.2% RevPAR index versus competitors.

Provides access to Qualified Opportunity Zone tax advantages.

partners.name: Hyatt Hotels Corporation
partners.description: Dual-branding partner for the PDX conversion, providing the JDV (Joie de Vivre) and Hyatt House brands.

advantages.title: Three Decades of Hospitality Experience
advantages.description: Vesta has been involved in the management of 47 hotel properties across the US and Canada since its founding in 1996.

advantages.title: Market-Leading Revenue Performance
advantages.description: In 2024, the owned portfolio performed at an average 123.2% RevPAR index compared to competitive sets, showcasing superior operational efficiency.

advantages.title: Local Sub-Market Expertise
advantages.description: The management team has over 20 years of specific experience operating hotels in the Portland Airport sub-market (Homewood Suites, Country Inn & Suites).

advantages.title: Results-Driven Operational Platform
advantages.description: Vesta provides a high level of immediate service through specialized expertise in personnel selection, marketing, and revenue management.
